content.problemStatement:
Chef has a tree with NNN nodes.

He is aware that adding an edge between any two nodes in the existing tree creates a cycle.

Chef has **two** extra edges. He wants to add each of two edges between two different nodes.

Count the number of ways this can be done so that the new cyclic paths do **not** share any **common edge or node**.

Consider the following tree for example:






One possible way to add the two edges is between nodes (1,5)(1,5)(1,5) and (3,4)(3,4)(3,4). The two cycles are  1−5−11 - 5 - 11−5−1 and 2−3−4−22 - 3  -  4  -  22−3−4−2. These cycles do not share any common edge or node.

.



Another possible way to add the two edges is between nodes (1,5)(1,5)(1,5) and (2,3)(2,3)(2,3). The two cycles are  1−5−11 - 5 - 11−5−1 and 2−3−22 - 3  -  22−3−2. These cycles do not share any common edge or node.

.



The following ways to add the edges are invalid since the cycles have common edges/nodes. Both trees contains cycles where 222 is a common node.

 


**Note**: Pairs of edges here are unordered. For example in the above tree, {(1,5),(2,4)},{(5,1),(2,4)},{(2,4),(1,5)},{(4,2),(5,1)}\{(1,5) , (2,4)\} ,  \{(5,1) , (2,4)\} , \{(2,4) , (1,5)\} , \{(4,2) , (5,1)\}{(1,5),(2,4)},{(5,1),(2,4)},{(2,4),(1,5)},{(4,2),(5,1)}.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN — the number of nodes in the tree.
The next N−1N-1N−1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two space-separated integers uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​, denoting an edge between uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of ways we can form two non-intersecting cyclic paths using two extra edges.


It can be proved that the answer fits within the limit of a signed 646464-bit integer.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 5⋅1055\cdot 10^55⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:  There is only one way to add the two edges such that cycles are non-intersecting. Add first edge between nodes (1,2)(1,2)(1,2) and second edge between (3,4)(3, 4)(3,4).
Test case 222: This has been explained in the problem statement. Possible ways to add edges are {(1,5),(2,4)},{(1,5),(2,3)},{(1,5),(3,4)}\{(1,5) , (2,4)\} ,  \{(1,5) , (2,3)\} , \{(1,5) , (3,4)\}{(1,5),(2,4)},{(1,5),(2,3)},{(1,5),(3,4)}.
